steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content: To solve $x^2 + x - 12 = 0$, factor the expression. We need two numbers that multiply to $-12$ and add to $1$. Those are $4$ and $-3$. $$x^2 + x - 12 = (x + 4)(x - 3) = 0$$

steps.title: Find the positive solution
steps.content: From the factored form $(x + 4)(x - 3) = 0$, we get $x = -4$ or $x = 3$. Since $a > 0$, the value of $a$ is $3$.
